inline_review:
commit 9f6937a3dc44330f7acb8f3868aa4691403877fe
Author: Hui Zhu <zhuhui@kylinos.cn>

mm/memcontrol: batch memcg charging in __memcg_slab_post_alloc_hook

This commit batches memcg charging by scanning ahead to find contiguous
runs of objects whose slabs share the same pgdat.

> diff --git a/mm/memcontrol.c b/mm/memcontrol.c
> --- a/mm/memcontrol.c
> +++ b/mm/memcontrol.c

[ ... ]

> +		/*
> +		 * The value of batch_bytes must not exceed
> +		 * (INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) to prevent integer overflow in
> +		 * the final accumulation performed by __account_obj_stock().
> +		 */
> +		max_size = min((size_t)((INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) / obj_size),
> +			       size);
> +
> +		for (j = i + 1; j < max_size; j++) {

Is the loop bound calculation for max_size missing the current index i?

If a bulk allocation is exceptionally large, max_size will be clamped to a
fixed limit. Once the outer loop index i reaches or exceeds this clamped
max_size, j will be initialized to i + 1, making j > max_size immediately.

Does this cause the inner loop to skip entirely, forcing all trailing
elements to be processed individually instead of in batches?

Should the bound be calculated relative to the current index, for example:
max_size = min(i + (size_t)((INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) / obj_size), size);
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Incorrect loop bound calculation for batch size limit completely breaks batching for trailing elements in large bulk allocations.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"The patch attempts to limit the accumulated `batch_bytes` to `INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE` to prevent integer overflow by calculating `max_size = min((size_t)((INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) / obj_size), size)`. However, `max_size` is then used as the upper bound for the absolute array index `j` (`for (j = i + 1; j < max_size; j++)`). If a bulk allocation requests a total size such that `size * obj_size > INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE` (i.e., > 2GB of memory in a single bulk allocation), `max_size` gets clamped to the maximum number of objects allowed in a batch. Once the outer loop index `i` reaches or exceeds this `max_size`, the inner loop condition `j < max_size` will immediately evaluate to false since `j = i + 1`. This forces all remaining elements in the array to be processed individually (`run_len = 1`), defeating the batching optimization for the trailing elements.

The correct logic should cap the bound relative to `i`, such as `min(size, i + (size_t)((INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) / obj_size))`. Because this logic error only manifests for exceptionally large bulk allocations (>2GB) which are extremely unlikely in practice, and the only consequence is a harmless fallback to the non-batched pre-patch behavior, the severity is Low."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 9f6937a3dc44330f7acb8f3868aa4691403877fe

If a bulk allocation is exceptionally large, max_size will be clamped to a
fixed limit. Once the outer loop index i reaches or exceeds this clamped

max_size = min(i + (size_t)((INT_MAX - PAGE_SIZE) / obj_size), size);
